code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test28-OCT-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria and glucosuria, with low specific gravity, suggesting renal tubular dysfunction. The urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but there is no evidence of infection (negative nitrites and leukocytes). These findings are consistent with possible chronic kidney disease and renal glycosuria; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
